topic.what: Foundational TCM clinical text establishing formula-based treatment for gastric disorders, including Ban Xia Xie Xin Tang.
topic.how: Systematic pattern-to-formula matching: specific symptom constellations map to specific multi-herb prescriptions.
topic.why_matters: Ban Xia Xie Xin Tang remains the most-studied classical TCM formula for gastritis and functional dyspepsia, with modern RCTs and mechanistic studies validating its efficacy.

characteristics.vs_baseline: Longest-used gastric prescription in any tradition

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of modern studies validating the formula
characteristics.value: Dozens of modern studies including RCTs and animal models
characteristics.context: Ban Xia Xie Xin Tang specifically

not_for: Western clinical practice without TCM training

novelty: Earliest protocol-driven approach to gastric disease treatment
Pattern-to-formula matching anticipates algorithmic clinical decision-making
Ban Xia Xie Xin Tang demonstrated efficacy against drug-resistant H. pylori in modern studies
open_problems: Formula requires TCM pattern diagnosis for correct application
Herb quality and standardization vary widely
Mechanism of multi-herb synergy not fully elucidated
